# TRANFU//AGENTS

> TRANFU//AGENTS is an open-source, self-hosted, vendor-neutral observability dashboard that shows in real time what every teammate's AI coding agent is doing — who is running which agent, the current step, the status, and how long it has been active. It works across Claude Code, Codex, Open Claw, Hermes, Manus, MuleRun, ChatGPT, and any CLI agent. It is MIT-licensed and deploys as a single container.

Key facts:
- Category: AI agent observability; team AI-agent monitoring dashboard.
- Deploy: a single Docker container (server + dashboard); no external services.
- Reporting: a lightweight "shim" emits a small JSON status event per agent.
- Identity model: operator (the person) → agent (a purpose label, e.g. "copy"/"code") → runtime (the tool).
- Heterogeneous: local CLI agents report step-level detail; cloud agents report start/end.
- Privacy: self-hosted; optional prompt/output capture is off by default.
- Schema: small, fully original event protocol (TATP); forward to any backend via a thin mapping layer.
- License: MIT. Home: https://tranfu.com

## Docs
- [README](https://github.com/tranfu-labs/tranfu-agents-app/blob/main/README.md): overview, supported agents, features, FAQ.
- [Deploy guide](https://github.com/tranfu-labs/tranfu-agents-app/blob/main/DEPLOY.md): self-host the server (admin).
- [Usage guide](https://github.com/tranfu-labs/tranfu-agents-app/blob/main/USAGE.md): how a teammate connects an agent in natural language.
- [Protocol](https://github.com/tranfu-labs/tranfu-agents-app/blob/main/PROTOCOL.md): the event schema and privacy posture.

## Optional
- [Skill](https://github.com/tranfu-labs/tranfu-agents-app/blob/main/SKILL.md): agent-readable self-install instructions.
